channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Grandmothers in Colombia get the quinceañera they never had
item.description: BOGOTÁ, Colombia (AP) — At age 72, Rosalba Casas finally celebrated her 15th birthday on Friday, donning a flowing pink dress and a tiara for the quinceañera she dreamed of as a teenager.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 5 shot near Howard University during homecoming week; 2 people in custody
item.description: Five people were hospitalized after a shooting near Howard University Friday, according to the DC Fire and EMS Department.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Dillon, ABC 4

item.title: Photos of Melodee Buzzard 'look like her': Aunt
item.description: Melodee's aunt, Lizabeth Meza, joined "Banfield" on Friday night and said she had not seen Melodee for over four years.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-abortion pregnancy centers are looking to offer much more than ultrasounds and diapers
item.description: More anti-abortion pregnancy centers have been offering medical services such as testing for sexually transmitted infections.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Early voting begins in New York City mayor's race and contest to pick New Jersey's next governor
item.description: Polling places open Saturday for in-person voting in two of the year’s most closely watched elections: The New York City mayor’s race and the contest to pick New Jersey’s next governor.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump’s Asia return and East Timor’s entry take spotlight in landmark ASEAN summit
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Southeast Asian foreign ministers opened talks Saturday ahead of a landmark ASEAN summit that will formally welcome East Timor as the bloc’s 11th member and mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dramatic video shows NYPD officers rescuing a missing dog named Moose from beneath a Manhattan subway train
item.description: NYPD officers rescued a small dog named Moose from beneath a Manhattan subway train at 14th Street station. Dramatic bodycam footage shows the heroic rescue mission.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shots fired, driver hurt and held after truck rams into Coast Guard base in California
item.description: A truck driver who backed into a U.S. Coast Guard base in the San Francisco Bay Area — the site of earlier protests against federal immigration agents — was shot by law enforcement officers and wounded, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration won't tap contingency fund to keep food aid flowing, memo says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is rejecting the idea of using roughly $5 billion in contingency funds to keep food aid flowing into November amid the government shutdown, according to a Department of Agriculture memo that surfaced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Walmart is changing how hourly store workers earn raises
item.description: The retail giant's new approach will take employee performance into account as well as years of service.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill to fund SNAP in shutdown introduced in House
item.description: Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R-Iowa) on Friday introduced a bill to fund the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) as the government shutdown threatens payments of the food aid in some states.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KRON4

item.title: Rosa Parks and Helen Keller statues unveiled at the Alabama Capitol
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Statues of Rosa Parks and Helen Keller, pivotal figures who fought for justice and inspired change across the world, were unveiled Friday on the grounds of the Alabama Capitol.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 7 charged in 2024 Pennsylvania voter registration fraud that prosecutors say was motivated by money
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A yearlong investigation into suspected fraudulent voter registration forms submitted ahead of last year's presidential election produced criminal charges Friday against six street canvassers and the man who led their work in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ prepares to send election monitors to California, New Jersey following requests from state GOPs
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Department of Justice is preparing to send federal election observers to California and New Jersey next month, targeting two Democratic states holding off-year elections following requests from state Republican parties.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pentagon accepts $130 million donation to help pay the military during the government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon confirmed Friday that it has accepted an anonymous $130 million gift to help pay members of the military during the government shutdown, raising ethical questions after President Donald Trump had announced that a friend had offered the gift to defray any shortfalls.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York adds $11M to hunger programs amid SNAP cuts, government shutdown
item.description: Governor Kathy Hochul announced $11 million in state funding for emergency food relief programs and urged the USDA to release $700 million in SNAP benefits to prevent a public health crisis, while the Fiscal Policy Institute suggested the state use its budget surplus to backfill the lost benefits.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: “In some instances, stopping your medicine may be more harmful to your health than continuing to take the recalled medicine,” the FDA says.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: New York man accused of killing estranged wife with cyanide-like powder
item.description: Aleena Asif's 18-year-old daughter called 911 because she was concerned when her mom didn't pick up her 7-year-old sister from school.
item.dc:creator: Mira Wassef, NewsNation

item.title: Judge halts death penalty case in New Mexico due to federal shutdown
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A judge in New Mexico has halted a federal death penalty case, citing the pinch of the U.S. government shutdown and a funding shortfall for specialized public defenders that stretches back to the summer.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Hispanics' views of Trump have changed since January, according to a new AP-NORC poll
item.description: President Donald Trump's favorability has fallen among Hispanic adults since the beginning of the year, a new AP-NORC poll shows, a potential warning sign from a key constituency that helped secure his victory in the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Poll: California's Prop 50 likely to pass, Republican surges in governor's race
item.description: With less than two weeks before election day, California voters appear poised to pass the state’s redistricting measure, Proposition 50, to counter Republican redistricting efforts in Texas to gain more seats in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9

item.title: NFL seeks dismissal of Jon Gruden's lawsuit after Nevada Supreme Court sends it back to state court
item.description: The Nevada Supreme Court returned Jon Gruden’s case to the state’s District Court on Thursday, and the NFL filed two motions seeking the prompt dismissal of the claims.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI AP Pro Football Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alaska Airlines operations resume after ground stop over IT outage
item.description: Alaska Airlines said its operations have resumed Friday after it issued a temporary ground stoppage Thursday evening amid an IT outage.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, WGN 9

item.title: Alaska Airlines resumes operations after an IT outage grounded its flights for hours
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Alaska Airlines said its operations have resumed Friday after it had to ground its planes for hours because of an information technology outage.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump-Kim meeting speculation flares ahead of US president's visit to South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The last time U.S. President Donald Trump visited South Korea in 2019, he made a surprise trip to the border with North Korea for an impromptu meeting with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un to revive faltering nuclear talks.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says tech leaders and mayor changed his mind about using federal force in San Francisco
item.description: President Donald Trump says he agreed to halt a planned show of federal force in San Francisco after appeals from tech executives and the mayor.
item.dc:creator: Janie Har, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NBA head coach and player charged in sprawling sports betting and Mafia-backed poker schemes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers and a player for the Miami Heat were arrested Thursday along with more than 30 other people in a takedown of two sprawling gambling operations that authorities said leaked inside information about NBA athletes and rigged poker games backed
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, TIM REYNOLDS and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As federal agents ramp up Chicago immigration crackdown, more elected officials caught in crosshairs
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Hoan Huynh was going door-to-door informing businesses of ramped up immigration enforcement on Chicago’s North Side when the Democratic state lawmaker got an activist notification of federal agents nearby.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bodycam footage of Jay Cutler's arrest released
item.description: Cutler, 41, was arrested in October 2024 after police responded to a minor traffic collision where he had rear-ended another vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KRON4

item.title: Trail Blazers’ Billups, Heat’s Rozier among dozens charged in Mafia-linked gambling probe
item.description: Portland Trail Blazers head coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier are among more than 30 people who have been charged in connection with schemes involving illegal sports betting and rigged poker games backed by Mafia, authorities said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Mamdani, Cuomo and Sliwa clash bitterly during final NYC mayoral debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zohran Mamdani was attacked over his thin resume, Republican Curtis Sliwa strove to prove his seriousness as a candidate and former Gov.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Unexpected colon cancer screening test show up at your door? This may be why
item.description: Did you receive a Cologuard test at your door, despite not ordering one? Someone may have ordered it for you.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Israel 'not a protectorate' of the US, Netanyahu says ahead of meeting with Vance
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel's prime minister toughened his stance Wednesday by declaring that his country is in charge of its own security and isn't an American protectorate as he prepared to discuss progress on Gaza’s fragile ceasefire agreement with U.S. Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Man taken into custody after driving car into White House gate: Secret Service
item.description: A man was taken into custody late Tuesday after driving his car into a portion of the White House security gate, a Secret Service official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Immigration agents conducting sweep on NYC's famed Canal Street confronted by protesters
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An immigration enforcement sweep targeting vendors on Manhattan’s famed Canal Street turned chaotic on Tuesday after droves of angry New Yorkers surrounded federal agents and attempted to block them from driving off, prompting arrests and fierce standoffs along a bustling downtown co
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pick to lead federal watchdog agency withdraws after offensive text messages were revealed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump ’s pick to lead a federal watchdog agency withdrew from consideration Tuesday evening, after his offensive text messages were made public and GOP senators revolted.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Former NFL player who died in police custody battled injuries and mental health challenges
item.description: Martin, 36, died after what police described as a “brief struggle” with officers who arrested him early Saturday while investigating a break-in at a home. He became unresponsive and was pronounced dead at a hospital, Oakland police said. A cause of death has not been released.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and MARK LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5
